ocr.text: The data presented in Table 1 provides critical insights into the performance metrics of the DGfield system during both
validation and training phases. These metrics are vital for assessing the efficacy of the system in real-world applications.
Specifically, the daily gain, indicated by the values under the "Training" and "Validation" columns, illustrates the system's
ability to adapt and improve over time.
Trait  Abbreviation  Complete pedigrees  Short pedigrees
Validation  Training  Validation  Training
Daily gain, field test records  DGfield  0.92  0.81  0.92  0.76
Daily gain, on station test records  DGstation  0.82  0.79  0.79  0.73
Carcass lean content, estimated with Bonner formulae  CLCBonn  0.76  0.78  0.68  0.70
Carcass lean content, FOM records  CLCFOM  0.90  0.78  0.88  0.72
Shoulder weight, AutoFOM records  SW  0.78  0.69  0.71  0.60
Belly weight, AutoFOM records  BW  0.77  0.69  0.71  0.61
Belly lean content, AutoFOM records  BLC  0.83  0.74  0.79  0.67
Ham weight, AutoFOM records  HW  0.79  0.72  0.73  0.65
Loin weight, AutoFOM records  LW  0.74  0.68  0.63  0.59
Loin eye area  LEA  0.71  0.76  0.58  0.68
Carcass length  CL  0.62  0.72  0.39  0.59
pH value, loin, 45 min. p.m.  pH1  0.60  0.70  0.25  0.52
Intramuscular fat content  IMF  0.55  0.53  0.22  0.36
Drip loss  Drip  0.59  0.67  0.24  0.50
For instance, the training daily gain achieved a noteworthy figure of 0.92, while the validation daily gain recorded a slightly
lower value of 0.81. This discrepancy suggests that while the model performs optimally with the training data, there may be
areas for enhancement when applied to validation scenarios. Such differences can be crucial for further refining the system and
ensuring its robustness in field tests. Additionally, the consistent performance across both phases indicates a level of reliability
that can be expected from DGfield in practical applications. It is essential to continue monitoring these metrics over time to
ascertain long-term performance trends and to implement necessary adjustments in the system's algorithms or training
techniques. Future analyses will focus on understanding the factors contributing to the observed validation training gap and
optimizing them accordingly.
